column2: As Mayor, you’re empowered to drive the narrative in #story_time. 📢\nWhen the team scales down (living players ≤ 66% of total headcount), the Mayor leverages an extra vote in polls to maximize decision-making ROI. 📈\nDuring high-growth phases (more players than that), the Mayor acts as a strategic visionary each night, deciding whether to double down with a second lynch for the following day. ⚖️
column3:
The Mayor’s extra vote is a non-negotiable asset—it cannot be nullified, stolen, or offshored under any circumstances. 🛡️
Alignment is key: If the Mayor’s primary vote is negative, the bonus vote pivots to negative as well.
Flexibility matters: The Mayor can choose to transition out of the role at any point, with the resignation finalized at the end of the phase. 🤝
Deciding on a double lynch is an agile, immediate action.
If the headcount hits that 2/3 threshold, the Mayor instantly unlocks the extra vote feature, sunsetting the double-lynch option. If a double lynch was already in the pipeline, we honor that commitment and the poll goes live. 🔄
The Mayor also gains access to an exclusive, legacy secret channel for cross-functional collaboration with future incumbents.
column4:
The Mayor is elected early in the talent acquisition cycle (before the reporter and guardian). They have exclusive posting rights in #story_time. ✍️
When the room is crowded (> 2/3 alive), they have the executive power to greenlight a second lynch.
When the team is leaner (≤ 2/3 alive), they pivot to a 2x voting power model in public polls. 💥 #Leadership #Strategy #DecisionMaking #Agile

column2: The Guardian is the ultimate protector of the community ecosystem. When the headcount hits that 66% threshold or lower, the Guardian scales their impact by securing one key stakeholder. column3:
The Guardian executes their protection strategy during the night cycle, ensuring stakeholders are shielded from external threats. 
- **Sustainability:** To maintain a balanced workflow, they can't protect the same individual two nights in a row. 
- **Self-Care & Agility:** They are empowered to protect themselves or pivot between multiple team members to maximize coverage. 🔄
- **Risk Mitigation:** While they successfully block direct attacks, they remain mindful that other variables can still impact a player's status.

_The Onboarding_ 🤝
The Guardian is headhunted on the first full day when the Mayor and Reporter are active and the position is vacant. 
- **Immediate Impact:** Protection goes live instantly. ⚡
- **Flexibility:** The Guardian has the autonomy to resign at the end of any phase. 
- **Networking:** They gain exclusive access to a private, high-level communication channel that stays active for all future successors. 📱
column4: The Guardian role is activated when the Mayor and Reporter are in office. Every night, they mitigate risk by shielding players from attacks. When the organization is at scale (>2/3 alive), they protect two stakeholders at once. #Leadership #Security #CommunityFirst #RiskManagement

column2: A Scarecrow is all about seamless **succession planning**. When they exit the game, they pass the torch to the next leader. 🤝

Crucially, alignment stays consistent—we’re not changing the mission, just the execution. 🛠️
**Agile Management:** Scarecrows can iterate on their target once per phase, with one **emergency pivot** available for high-stakes situations. 🚨 If the strategy doesn't align, they can opt-out of targeting altogether to pause the workflow. 🛑

column2: Every night, the Zombie has the opportunity to \onboard\" another player. If a player is engaged two nights in a row
column3: they officially join the Zombie team."
column4:
When a Zombie connects with another player, they become “bitten.“ The player remains unaware of this initial touchpoint. If a Zombie follows up the very next night, that player immediately scales into a Zombie role. 
Biting is an immediate, high-impact effect. ⚡️
Synergy is key: If a Zombie exits the game, every other Zombie they personally recruited also exits. 📉
The win condition? When the entire market is either out of the game or has pivoted to the graveyard team. 🏆
Zombies have exclusive access to the graveyard channel, ensuring full transparency with other Zombies and corpses. They can even network with other members of the graveyard channel.
When a player transitions into a Zombie, they pivot from their original team to join the graveyard channel. There is a brief cooling-off period before they can bite another player. 
They leave their previous role behind, but if they were part of a legacy group like the wolfpack, they maintain their seat at the table and can still participate in the conversation (though their vote no longer carries weight) as long as one legacy member remains. 
If a group loses all its original members to role changes or exits, the group is officially disbanded, and all associated perks are lost. 🚫

_Edge Cases_ 💡
If a Zombie undergoes a career pivot and changes their role, the dependency on their creator is severed—they won't exit when their creator does, nor will their own recruits be affected.
If a Zombie attempts to recruit a member of the hell team (second bite), the strategy backfires, and the Zombie pivots into an imp role. Since hell team members are non-convertible, they can be \engaged" multiple times without ever joining the graveyard team."
column5: Every night, the Zombie engages another player. Two consecutive touchpoints result in a successful conversion to the Zombie team and graveyard channel. Success is defined by total market saturation (dead or graveyard team). \nNote: Leadership exits result in a downstream impact where all direct recruits also exit. #Networking #GrowthMindset #ZombieLife
